# RenderCV

> The open-source CV/resume builder for academics and engineers. Perfect typesetting — just write YAML, get a PDF.

Website: https://rendercv.com
Documentation: https://docs.rendercv.com
GitHub: https://github.com/rendercv/rendercv
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/rendercv
Pricing: https://rendercv.com/pricing

## What is RenderCV?

RenderCV is a CV/resume builder that turns structured YAML input into professionally typeset PDFs. It is designed for academics, engineers, and researchers who want clean, consistent CVs without wrestling with LaTeX or Word templates.

RenderCV exists in three forms:
1. A web app at rendercv.com — edit in the browser with a live PDF preview, no installation needed.
2. A Python CLI tool — install via `pip install "rendercv[full]"`, write YAML in any text editor, run `rendercv render cv.yaml` to get a PDF.
3. A Python library — import rendercv in Python for programmatic CV generation.

All three share the same YAML schema, themes, and rendering pipeline. A CV written for the CLI works in the web app and vice versa.

## Why RenderCV?

- Version-control your CV — it's just a text file (YAML).
- Focus on content — formatting is handled automatically.
- Perfect typography — consistent alignment, spacing, and layout across every page.
- Strict validation — if something's wrong in your YAML, you get a clear error message telling you exactly what and where.
- No vendor lock-in — your data is a plain YAML file you own.

## How It Works

The rendering pipeline is: YAML → Typst → PDF.

1. Your CV content is written as YAML — a simple, human-readable structured format.
2. RenderCV validates the YAML using Pydantic (strict sc hema validation with clear error messages).
3. Jinja2 templates generate a Typst source file from your data and chosen theme.
4. The Typst compiler (a modern typesetting system) produces the final PDF.

In the web app, this entire pipeline runs client-side in the browser using WebAssembly (Pyodide for Python + Typst WASM compiler). No server processing is needed for PDF generation.

## The Web App (rendercv.com)

The web app provides two editing modes:
- Form editor — a structured visual interface with drag-and-drop reordering, collapsible entries, a color picker, dimension inputs, and a font selector.
- YAML editor — a code editor with autocomplete, schema validation, and error markers.

A live PDF preview updates in real time as you edit.

### Web App Features

- Live PDF preview beside the editor
- AI-powered chat assistant for CV writing, editing, and content suggestions
- PDF import — upload an existing resume and extract its content automatically via AI
- Multiple CV files — manage several CV versions in one place
- Public sharing — generate a shareable link to a rendered CV (Plus tier)
- GitHub sync — auto-sync all CVs as YAML files to a GitHub repository on every save (Pro tier)
- Download as PDF, Typst source, or ZIP archive
- Keyboard shortcuts (undo/redo, bold, italic, file management)
- Works entirely in the browser — no installation required

### Web App Pricing

| Tier | Storage | AI Credits/Month | Key Features |
|------|---------|------------------|--------------|
| Free | Browser (localStorage) | 1 | Editing + PDF export |
| Plus | Cloud | 10 | Cloud storage, public sharing, PDF import |
| Pro  | Cloud | 50 | GitHub sync, all features |

## The CLI Tool

Install (requires Python 3.12+):
```
pip install "rendercv[full]"
```

Create a new CV:
```
rendercv new "John Doe"
```

Edit the YAML file in any text editor, then render:
```
rendercv render John_Doe_CV.yaml
```

The CLI also supports JSON Schema for autocompletion and inline documentation in editors like VS Code.

## Themes

Five built-in themes, each with different visual defaults but all fully customizable:
- classic
- engineeringclassic
- engineeringresumes
- moderncv
- sb2nov

All themes share the same customization options: colors, fonts, page margins, spacing, entry layout templates, header style, footer, and more.

## YAML Schema

A RenderCV YAML file has four top-level fields:

```yaml
cv:        # Your CV content — name, contact info, sections (required)
design:    # Visual styling — theme, colors, fonts, margins (optional)
locale:    # Language and date formatting (optional)
settings:  # Rendering configuration (optional)
```

### Supported Entry Types

RenderCV auto-detects the entry type from the fields you use:
- EducationEntry — institution, area, degree, dates, location, highlights
- ExperienceEntry — company, position, dates, location, summary, highlights
- NormalEntry — name, dates, location, summary, highlights (for projects, volunteering, etc.)
- PublicationEntry — title, authors, journal, DOI, date
- OneLineEntry — label and details on a single line (for skills, languages, etc.)
- BulletEntry — simple bullet points
- TextEntry — plain text paragraphs
- NumberedEntry / ReversedNumberedEntry — auto-numbered lists

### Text Formatting

Markdown is supported in all text fields: **bold**, *italic*, [links](url), and nested bullet points.

### Date Handling

Dates accept YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM, or YYYY formats. Use `start_date` and `end_date` for ranges, `end_date: present` for ongoing items, or `date` for single events. Arbitrary text like "Fall 2023" is also accepted.

### Locales

20+ languages are supported including English, French, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Arabic, Turkish, and more. The locale controls date formatting, month names, and phrases like "present" or "to".

## Design Customization

Every visual aspect is configurable through the `design` field:
- Page: size (us-letter, a4), margins (top, bottom, left, right), footer, top note
- Colors: name, headlines, section titles, links, body text, footer (accepts hex, RGB, HSL, or SVG color names)
- Typography: font family (20+ bundled fonts), line spacing, alignment, date column alignment
- Entry templates: control the layout of each entry type using placeholder-based templates

## Technology

- Web app: SvelteKit, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, deployed on Cloudflare Workers
- Rendering: Pyodide (Python in WebAssembly) + Typst (WASM compiler) — all client-side
- CLI/Library: Python 3.12+, Pydantic, Jinja2, ruamel.yaml, typst-py
- Open source: MIT license
